summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #551 where the 'Detect All Models' button in Moltis failed to discover new models added to a provider after startup.
summary.outcome: Implemented a rediscovery phase in detect_supported() that re-queries provider APIs (/v1/models) before probing, adds newly discovered models to the registry, and wired it into LiveModelService via a new with_discovery_config() builder. All 307 tests pass and all three modified crates pass clippy cleanly.

repo: Model discovery (calling /v1/models) only runs at startup via ProviderRegistry::fire_discoveries(). The 'Detect All Models' path (detect_supported) was probe-only and never re-queried provider APIs.
fire_discoveries() spawns OS threads with block_on, making it unsuitable for async contexts. The fix uses fetch_models_from_api directly, which is already async.
The registry write lock must not be held across async HTTP fetches — the fix explicitly splits the fetch phase (no lock) from the registration phase (brief write lock).
detect_supported() is user-initiated and gated by a semaphore, so the extra network calls per provider during rediscovery are acceptable.
refresh_dynamic_source_models (used for OAuth providers like Codex/Copilot) was the reference pattern for incremental model registration.
OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS and custom providers both need to be handled in rediscovery, requiring access to ProvidersConfig and env_overrides.
crates/chat already depends on both moltis-config and moltis-providers, so no new Cargo.toml dependencies were needed.
The workspace has a CUDA/llama-cpp build issue that causes workspace-wide clippy/test to fail; scoping clippy to specific crates is required.

code.finding: detect_supported() probe loop — this is where the rediscovery 'Phase 0' was inserted before model probing begins.
register_rediscovered_models() must be pub (not pub(crate)) to be callable from moltis-chat crate.

workflow: Split async operations that hold locks into fetch (no lock) + register (brief lock) phases to avoid blocking readers during network I/O.
When clippy flags nested if-let patterns, collapsing with && requires careful brace counting — running cargo fmt immediately after the edit helps catch mismatched braces.
Running cargo check on individual crates (not the full workspace) is the reliable path when the workspace has environment-specific build failures (e.g., CUDA).
The beads issue tracker was used to claim and close the work item alongside the GitHub issue.

friction: Workspace-wide clippy/cargo check fails due to a CUDA/llama-cpp build environment issue unrelated to the changes, requiring per-crate compilation checks throughout.
Nested if-let collapse for clippy lint caused a mismatched brace bug that required re-reading and re-editing the affected block multiple times.
Unicode/whitespace in Edit tool old_string caused a match failure on one edit attempt, requiring a re-read before retrying.
The initial rediscover_models implementation held the write lock across async HTTP fetches — caught and refactored after the fact rather than designed correctly upfront.
Had to iterate through multiple Grep queries to locate fire_discoveries, collect_discoveries, and related startup plumbing before understanding the full discovery lifecycle.
open_items: No integration test was added verifying that detect_supported() finds models added after initial startup (noted in the plan but only unit tests were implemented).
provider_filter scoping (rediscovering only a single provider's models rather than all) was mentioned in the plan but not implemented — rediscovery currently re-queries all eligible providers.

The rediscover_models convenience method on ProviderRegistry is now unused internally (the chat crate calls the split fetch+register approach directly) — may trigger a dead_code warning in future or could be removed.
